stem: What is the current basic rate of Income Tax in the UK?
explanation: The basic rate of Income Tax in the UK is 20%, applicable to income within the basic rate band.

stem: At what rate is the additional rate of Income Tax charged?
explanation: The additional rate of Income Tax is 45%, applying to taxable income above the additional rate threshold.

stem: Employer contributions to a registered group life scheme are normally treated for tax purposes as
options: a benefit in kind for the employee.
an allowable business expense for the employer.
a personal gift from the employer.
explanation: Employer contributions to a registered group life scheme are treated as an allowable business expense, deductible against Corporation Tax profits.

stem: For what purpose is a P11D form primarily used?
options: To report an employee's annual earnings to HMRC.
To report benefits in kind and expenses to HMRC.
To claim tax relief on pension contributions.
To register a group scheme with HMRC.
explanation: The P11D form is used by employers to report benefits in kind and expenses provided to employees to HMRC.

stem: Under the PAYE system, the employer is responsible for deducting Income Tax and
explanation: Under PAYE, the employer deducts both Income Tax and National Insurance contributions from the employee's pay before paying them.

stem: Benefits received under a group income protection policy by an employee are subject to
options: Income Tax and National Insurance contributions.
explanation: Benefits paid under a group income protection scheme are treated as employment income and are therefore subject to both Income Tax and National Insurance contributions via PAYE.

stem: The standard rate of Insurance Premium Tax (IPT) is currently
explanation: The standard rate of Insurance Premium Tax is 12%. It applies to most general insurance premiums but group life and group income protection premiums are generally exempt.

stem: Which of the following group risk benefits is generally exempt from Insurance Premium Tax?
explanation: Group life assurance (long-term insurance) is generally exempt from Insurance Premium Tax. IPT applies to general insurance contracts such as group private medical insurance and group critical illness.

stem: An employer pays the premium on a group income protection scheme. For the employee, this premium is treated as
options: a benefit in kind subject to Income Tax.
earnings subject to National Insurance only.
explanation: Employer-paid premiums for group income protection are not treated as a benefit in kind for the employee. Instead, any benefit subsequently paid is taxed as earnings.

stem: The UK tax year runs from
explanation: The UK tax year runs from 6 April in one year to 5 April in the following year.

stem: A lump-sum death benefit paid from a registered group life scheme within two years of the member's death is normally
explanation: A lump-sum death benefit paid from a registered pension scheme within two years of the scheme administrator becoming aware of the death is normally tax-free, provided it does not exceed the lump sum and death benefit allowance.

stem: Employer contributions to a group income protection scheme can normally be offset against profits for
explanation: Employer contributions to a group income protection scheme are treated as an allowable business expense and can be offset against profits for Corporation Tax purposes.

stem: What is the deadline for submitting a paper self-assessment tax return to HMRC?
options: 31 January following the end of the tax year.
5 April following the end of the tax year.
31 July following the end of the tax year.
31 October following the end of the tax year.
explanation: The deadline for a paper self-assessment tax return is 31 October following the end of the tax year. The online deadline is 31 January.

stem: An excepted group life scheme is one where the lump-sum benefit on death does not exceed
explanation: An excepted group life policy traditionally provided benefits not exceeding four times salary without needing to be registered as a pension scheme. This was distinct from registered schemes.

stem: Which type of trust arrangement helps ensure that a lump-sum death benefit avoids Inheritance Tax?
explanation: A discretionary trust is commonly used for group death-in-service schemes. Because the trustees have discretion over who receives the benefit, the lump sum does not form part of the deceased's estate and so avoids Inheritance Tax.

stem: The higher rate of Income Tax in the UK is currently
explanation: The higher rate of Income Tax is 40%, applicable to taxable income above the basic rate band but below the additional rate threshold.

stem: Employer-paid premiums for a group critical illness scheme are generally treated as
options: a benefit in kind for the employee, subject to Income Tax.
an allowable business expense not taxable on the employee.
explanation: Unlike group life and group income protection, employer-paid premiums for group critical illness are typically treated as a benefit in kind, meaning the employee is liable for Income Tax on the premium cost.

stem: Which of the following is subject to Insurance Premium Tax?
explanation: Group critical illness insurance is a general insurance product and is therefore subject to Insurance Premium Tax. Group life and group income protection are long-term insurance contracts and are exempt.

stem: The annual allowance for pension contributions, above which a tax charge applies, is currently
explanation: The annual allowance for pension contributions is currently £60,000. Contributions exceeding this amount are subject to an annual allowance charge.

stem: A lump-sum benefit paid under an excepted group life policy is
options: exempt from Income Tax but may be subject to Inheritance Tax.
explanation: Benefits from an excepted group life policy are exempt from Income Tax. However, unless written under a suitable trust arrangement (typically discretionary), they could be subject to Inheritance Tax if they form part of the deceased's estate.
